page: Microsoft Azure E48s_v7 is an Esv7-family virtual machine configured with 48 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. The instance is powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, featuring 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled (2.0 threads per core) and a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. It operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor with dedicated CPU allocation. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data shows strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 25%. Single-threaded compression and decompression workloads perform in the top 10%, with gzip compression reaching 36.68 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression reaching 15.04 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and uncached large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks falling into the middle 50%. The hardware-assisted matrix extensions contribute to top-tier prompt processing and text generation speeds for small language models, while medium and large model inference speeds remain in the top 25% and middle 50% respectively.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E48s_v7 offers a high-density memory allocation relative to its compute resources, making it suitable for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of higher vCPU tiers. The absence of local storage requires external disk provisioning, which should be factored into overall architecture planning. Given its memory capacity, strong single-core execution, and high database operation benchmarks (top 25% in PassMark database and Redis SET operations), this server is suited for relational databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise application servers, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E48s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture with 24 physical cores and hyper-threading, it includes 480 MB of L3 cache and supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instructions. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance, database operations, and Redis SET throughput, alongside top-tier single-threaded compression and small-model LLM inference speeds. Multi-core CPU scaling and large-block memory bandwidth fall within average ranges. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive workloads, including relational databases, in-memory caches, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48s_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 6973P-C CPU. It features strong single-core performance and is designed for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48s_v7 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, suited for databases and memory-bound workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, advanced matrix extensions, and high memory-to-core density for enterprise databases.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM providing an 8:1 memory-to-core ratio
Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 4.2 GHz clock speed and 480 MB L3 cache
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support accelerating CPU-based LLM inference
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds
No local storage bundled, requiring external Premium Storage provisioning
Suited for relational databases, Redis caching, and memory-bound enterprise applications
